name: Route of the Three Villas – running circular from Huecas through Rielves and Castillo de Barcience
name_original: Ruta de las Tres Villas – running circular desde Huecas por Rielves y Castillo de Barcience
region_intro: Jogging routes around Torrijos offer a variety of options across a landscape characterized by mostly paved surfaces and gently rolling terrain. The region features diverse routes with varying elevation gains, suitable for different fitness levels. These paths often connect towns and pass through agricultural areas, providing accessible running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Torrijos The most popular jogging route is Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a total ascent of 361 feet (110 meters). Another top favourite among local runners is Route of the Three Villas – running circular from Huecas through Rielves and Castillo de Barcience, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route connects several towns and offers a total ascent of 311 feet (95 meters). Local runners also love the Torrijos Town Center loop from Torrijos, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the town center, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Torrijos is defined by paved paths, agricultural landscapes, and routes connecting local towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate runs. The routes in Torrijos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 260 runners have used komoot to explore Torrijos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes are available in Torrijos?
A: Running routes around Torrijos primarily feature mostly paved surfaces and gently rolling terrain. You'll find a variety of paths connecting towns and passing through agricultural areas, suitable for different fitness levels. There are over 50 routes available, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Torrijos?
A: Yes, Torrijos offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrón Viewpoint – Enebros Viewpoint loop from Río Las Cabreras&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km (2.8 miles) route with moderate elevation gain, offering a pleasant experience for those new to running.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Torrijos area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Torrijos are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021191768/barrancas-de-burujon-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021452274/route-of-the-three-villas-running-circular-from-huecas-through-rielves-and-castillo-de-barcience" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Villas – running circular from Huecas through Rielves and Castillo de Barcience&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while jogging in Torrijos?
A: While jogging in Torrijos, you can encounter several interesting sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021452274/route-of-the-three-villas-running-circular-from-huecas-through-rielves-and-castillo-de-barcience" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Villas&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de Barcience&lt;/strong&gt;. You can also enjoy scenic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cambrón&lt;/a&gt;, which offer vistas of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Castrejón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any running routes that go through the town center of Torrijos?
A: Yes, there is a dedicated route that takes you through the heart of Torrijos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center loop from Torrijos&lt;/a&gt; is a 10 km (6.2 miles) moderate trail that allows you to experience the town's atmosphere while running.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Torrijos?
A: The running routes in Torrijos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the accessibility of the paths, and the pleasant experience of connecting different towns and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are the running routes in Torrijos suitable for families or those with dogs?
A: Many of the routes around Torrijos, particularly those on paved surfaces and through agricultural areas, are generally suitable for families. For specific dog-friendly information, it's always best to check local regulations or route descriptions, but the open nature of many paths often accommodates runners with dogs.

Q: What is the typical distance and elevation gain for running routes in Torrijos?
A: The routes vary significantly in distance and elevation. You can find shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torrijos&lt;/a&gt; at 6.8 km (4.2 miles) with minimal elevation, up to longer runs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31885150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Portillo de Toledo&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 13.5 km (8.4 miles) with around 66 meters (216 feet) of ascent. Most routes feature moderate elevation changes.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Torrijos?
A: Parking availability varies by route. For trails like those around the Barrancas de Burujón, there is a designated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking area at Las Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/a&gt;. For routes starting from town centers, street parking or public car parks would typically be available.

Q: Are there any running routes near other towns or villages around Torrijos?
A: Yes, the network of routes often connects various towns and villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt; offers a route starting from a nearby town, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Valdemoro&lt;/a&gt; provides another option further afield, showcasing the regional connectivity of the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Torrijos?
A: Torrijos, Spain, generally offers pleasant conditions for jogging during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite hot, making early morning or late evening runs preferable. Winter offers cooler temperatures, which can be ideal for longer runs, though occasional rain might occur.
tips:
We can tour its large courtyard, and the almost square plan that it presents, indicating by its size and the time in which it was erected, that it served as a residence rather than a defensive fort. Two interesting cubes are indications of the barrier and the moat that protected the entrance door to the enclosure, and of which there are no remains. Three towers, two circular, another square, and the homage, make up the rest of the enclosure.

Juan de Silva, the first Count of Cifuentes, began the construction of the fortress in the 14th century, which would be completed by his grandson, with the same name. It was endowed with artillery and garrison in the 16th century.
